title: Every basic commutator of weight > c lies in N_c

comments.author: human
comments.body: By Hall's collection process, $\gamma_c(F)$ is generated as a subgroup by ALL basic commutators of weight $\ge c$. The weight-$c$ ones lie in $N_c$ by definition. The nontrivial content is that every basic commutator of weight $w>c$ also lies in $N_c=\langle B_c\rangle^F$. This is NOT termwise obvious: a basic commutator $[u,v]$ of weight $w>c$ can be built from sub-commutators $u,v$ both of weight $<c$ (the first such case is weight $4$ in rank $\ge3$, e.g. $[[x_2,x_1],[x_3,x_1]]$ relative to $c=3$), so it is not visibly a commutator of a weight-$c$ element. A proof by induction on weight $w\ge c$ must rewrite $[u,v]$ mod $\gamma_{w+1}$ as a $\mathbb Z$-combination of weight-$w$ basic commutators and control the weight-$(w+1)$ correction tail; care is needed since the tail is infinite while each weight class is finite, so the induction should be organised inside the finite quotients $F/\gamma_m$.
